title: Patagonia
tagline: Wild, wind-swept landscapes of glaciers, jagged peaks, and vast open silence.
why_go: See the iconic granite towers of Torres del Paine rise dramatically
Walk alongside vast glaciers like Perito Moreno
Experience remote and untouched landscapes far from crowds
essential_experiences: Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk and boat trip
El Chaltén & Fitz Roy day hikes
seasonality_note: Best hiking conditions in summer (Dec–Feb), but wind is constant year-round.
food_and_drink: Simple but hearty meals in refugios on the trail
Great cafés and restaurants in El Calafate
Vegetarian options limited in remote areas — plan ahead
logistics: Fly via Santiago or Buenos Aires, then domestic flight to Punta Arenas or El Calafate
Buses and pre-booked transfers are the easiest way to get around
Accommodation ranges from refugios to hotels and Airbnbs
Park passes required — book online well in advance
friction_factors: Very long travel time from Australia (30+ hours each way)
Highly unpredictable weather — four seasons in one day is common
Expensive accommodation inside the national parks
Limited vegetarian options in remote trail areas
tips: Book W Trek refugios 6–12 months in advance for Jan/Feb
Pack full waterproofs, layers, and sun protection — all needed daily
Border crossings between Chile and Argentina can take 1–2 hours

water_safety.label: Generally Safe
water_safety.note: Tap water is safe in major towns. Check locally in remote backcountry.

itinerary.label: Enter Torres del Paine, W Trek begins
Exit park, rest in Puerto Natales
Day trip to El Chaltén / Fitz Roy

verdict: A true nature escape. Remote, dramatic, and incredibly rewarding if you're willing to put in the effort. The scale of the landscapes is unlike anywhere else — nothing quite prepares you for it.
